title_en: As-Suyuti and the Comprehensive Guide to Hadith Sciences
content_en:
Jalal ad-Din Abd ar-Rahman ibn Abi Bakr as-Suyuti was born in Cairo in 849 AH (1445 CE) and died there in 911 AH (1505 CE). He is one of the most prolific scholars in the history of Islamic civilization, credited with authorship of over five hundred works spanning Quranic sciences, hadith, jurisprudence, Arabic language, history, and many other disciplines. His extraordinary productivity reflected not only immense intelligence and energy but a distinctive scholarly method: he was a master synthesizer who could identify, organize, and present the best of the prior tradition in clear and comprehensive form.

As-Suyuti's Tadrib ar-Rawi fi Sharh Taqrib Al-Nawawi ('The Training of the Transmitter: Commentary on Nawawi's Taqrib') is his comprehensive treatise on hadith sciences, organized as a commentary on Al-Nawawi's shorter work Taqrib at-Tawfiq. Like many of as-Suyuti's works, it is encyclopedic: it draws on the full range of prior scholarship in hadith sciences — from the Muqaddimah of Ibn as-Salah through the works of Ibn Hajar — and synthesizes them into a single comprehensive reference. Students who study Tadrib ar-Rawi gain access to the accumulated wisdom of the hadith sciences tradition across several centuries.

As-Suyuti's formation in hadith sciences was thorough. He studied under the leading hadith scholars of his era in Cairo, memorized vast numbers of hadith, and accumulated the broad chains of transmission that qualified him to speak with authority on matters of hadith criticism. His teacher Siraj ad-Din al-Bulqini recognized his extraordinary talent and gave him responsibilities unusual for someone so young. By his mid-twenties, as-Suyuti was teaching independently and producing original scholarly works.

The Tadrib ar-Rawi covers the standard topics of hadith sciences in the organization established by Ibn as-Salah's Muqaddimah: the classification of hadith by quality, the biography and criticism of narrators, the identification of defects in transmission chains, the terminology of hadith scholarship, and the principles governing the collection and authentication of hadith. As-Suyuti's treatment of each topic is more comprehensive than any single earlier work had provided, drawing on multiple earlier discussions and resolving their differences where possible.

His distinctive contribution to the field was the synthesis itself: bringing together the insights of Ibn as-Salah, al-Iraqi, Ibn Hajar, and other major figures of hadith sciences into a single organized reference. This synthesis was more than mere compilation; it required the scholarly judgment to evaluate competing positions, identify areas of genuine consensus, and present the integrated result clearly.
title_id: As-Suyuti dan Panduan Komprehensif Ilmu Hadis
content_id:
Jalal ad-Din Abd ar-Rahman ibn Abi Bakr as-Suyuti lahir di Kairo pada tahun 849 H (1445 M) dan wafat di sana pada tahun 911 H (1505 M). Ia adalah salah satu ulama paling produktif dalam sejarah peradaban Islam, dikreditkan dengan pengarangan lebih dari lima ratus karya yang mencakup ilmu Quran, hadis, yurisprudensi, bahasa Arab, sejarah, dan banyak disiplin lainnya. Produktivitasnya yang luar biasa mencerminkan bukan hanya kecerdasan dan energi yang luar biasa tetapi juga metode ilmiah yang khas: ia adalah master sintesis yang dapat mengidentifikasi, mengorganisir, dan menyajikan yang terbaik dari tradisi sebelumnya dalam bentuk yang jelas dan komprehensif.

Tadrib ar-Rawi fi Syarh Taqrib an-Nawawi ('Melatih Perawi: Syarh atas Taqrib al-Nawawi') karya as-Suyuti adalah risalahnya yang komprehensif tentang ilmu hadis, disusun sebagai syarh atas karya al-Nawawi yang lebih pendek, Taqrib at-Tawfiq. Seperti banyak karya as-Suyuti, ia bersifat ensiklopedis: ia memanfaatkan rangkaian lengkap kajian ilmiah sebelumnya dalam ilmu hadis — dari Muqaddimah Ibn as-Salah hingga karya-karya Ibn Hajar — dan mensintesiskannya menjadi satu referensi komprehensif tunggal. Para pelajar yang mempelajari Tadrib ar-Rawi mendapatkan akses ke kebijaksanaan yang terakumulasi dari tradisi ilmu hadis selama beberapa abad.

Formasi as-Suyuti dalam ilmu hadis sangat menyeluruh. Ia belajar di bawah ulama-ulama hadis terkemuka di eranya di Kairo, menghafal hadis dalam jumlah besar, dan mengumpulkan sanad-sanad yang luas yang memenuhi syaratnya untuk berbicara dengan otoritas tentang masalah-masalah kritik hadis. Gurunya, Siraj ad-Din al-Bulqini, mengakui bakatnya yang luar biasa dan memberinya tanggung jawab yang tidak biasa bagi seseorang semuda itu. Pada pertengahan dua puluh tahunannya, as-Suyuti sudah mengajar secara mandiri dan menghasilkan karya-karya ilmiah orisinal.

Tadrib ar-Rawi mencakup topik-topik standar ilmu hadis dalam organisasi yang ditetapkan oleh Muqaddimah Ibn as-Salah: klasifikasi hadis berdasarkan kualitas, biografi dan kritik perawi, identifikasi cacat dalam sanad, terminologi kajian hadis, dan prinsip-prinsip yang mengatur pengumpulan dan otentikasi hadis. Perlakuan as-Suyuti terhadap setiap topik lebih komprehensif dari yang pernah diberikan oleh karya tunggal mana pun sebelumnya, memanfaatkan berbagai diskusi sebelumnya dan menyelesaikan perbedaan-perbedaannya sedapat mungkin.

Kontribusi khasnya pada bidang ini adalah sintesis itu sendiri: menyatukan wawasan-wawasan Ibn as-Salah, al-'Iraqi, Ibn Hajar, dan tokoh-tokoh utama ilmu hadis lainnya menjadi satu referensi yang terorganisir. Sintesis ini lebih dari sekadar kompilasi; ia membutuhkan penilaian ilmiah untuk mengevaluasi posisi-posisi yang bersaing, mengidentifikasi area-area konsensus yang tulus, dan menyajikan hasil integrasinya dengan jelas.
content_ar: Jalal ad-Din Abd ar-Rahman ibn Abi Bakr as-Suyuti was born in Cairo in 849 AH (1445 CE) and died there in 911 AH (1505 CE). He is one of the most prolific العلماء in the history of Islamic civilization, credited with authorship of over five hundred works spanning القرآني sciences, الحديث, jurisprudence, Arabic language, history, and many other disciplines. His extraordinary productivity reflected not only immense intelligence and energy but a distinctive العلمي method: he was a master synthesizer who could identify, organize, and present the best of the prior التراث in clear and comprehensive form. As-Suyuti's Tadrib ar-Rawi fi Sharh Taqrib النووي ('The Training of the Transmitter: Commentary on Nawawi's Taqrib') is his comprehensive treatise on الحديث sciences, organized as a commentary on النووي's shorter work Taqrib at-Tawfiq. Like many of as-Suyuti's works, it is encyclopedic: it draws on the full range of prior scholarship in الحديث sciences — from the Muqaddimah of Ibn as-الصلاة through the works of Ibn Hajar — and synthesizes them into a single comprehensive reference. Students who study Tadrib ar-Rawi gain access to the accumulated الحكمة of the الحديث sciences التراث across several centuries. As-Suyuti's formation in الحديث sciences was thorough. He studied under the leading الحديث العلماء of his era in Cairo, memorized vast numbers of الحديث, and accumulated the broad chains of transmission that qualified him to speak with authority on matters of الحديث criticism. His teacher Siraj ad-Din al-Bulqini recognized his extraordinary talent and gave him responsibilities unusual for someone لذا young. By his mid-twenties, as-Suyuti was teaching independently and producing original العلمي works. The Tadrib ar-Rawi covers the standard topics of الحديث sciences in the organization established by Ibn as-الصلاة's Muqaddimah: the classification of الحديث by quality, the biography and criticism of narrators, the identification of defects in transmission chains, the terminology of الحديث scholarship, and the principles governing the collection and authentication of الحديث. As-Suyuti's treatment of each topic is more comprehensive than any single earlier work had provided, drawing on multiple earlier discussions and resolving their differences where possible. His distinctive contribution to the field was the synthesis itself: bringing together the insights of Ibn as-الصلاة, al-Iraqi, Ibn Hajar, and other major figures of الحديث sciences into a single organized reference. This synthesis was more than mere compilation; it required the العلمي judgment to evaluate competing positions, identify areas of genuine consensus, and present the integrated result clearly.
